title: Microwave Steamed Eggs
summary: Inspired by Japanese chawanmushi, the eggs are steamed in the microwave until set, silky and custardy. Topped with chilli crisp and spring onions. Eaten with rice for a gentle and quick dinner.

method.description: Crack the eggs into a bowl. Whisk together with the dashi or stock, soy sauce, mirin and sugar. Place a strainer over another bowl and strain the egg mixture through – this will ensure silky eggs. Thinly slice the spring onion.

Pour the eggs into a large enough microwave-safe bowl, remove any foamy bits and put in the microwave. Cook the eggs on 500W for 7-10 mins. After 5 mins, the custard should start setting. Cook in bursts of 30 seconds until ready.
Drizzle the silky eggs with some chilli crisp and the soy sauce, then scatter the spring onions on top.

chefs.name: Chloe René
chefs.summary: Chloe (@chlo_eat) takes inspiration from her diverse upbringing to create recipes and dishes influenced from cultures around the world. Despite her classical training, Chloe's philosophy in the kitchen revolves around simplicity and home-cooking. She firmly believes that the essence of a great meal lies in the quality of ingredients and the care put into their preparation. Instead of relying on complex and technical processes, Chloe prizes the art of letting ingredients shine through, enhancing their natural flavours with a delicate touch.
